title: Serverless Invocation Cost Trending
app: Cloud billing with serverless product codes
spl:
index=cloud_billing sourcetype="aws:billing:cur" earliest=-30d
| search lineItem_ProductCode IN ("AWSLambda","AmazonSNS") OR lineItem_UsageType="*Lambda*"
| eval cost=tonumber(lineItem_UnblendedCost)
| timechart span=1d sum(cost) as serverless_cost sum(lineItem_UsageAmount) as units
description: Daily Lambda/Azure Functions/Google Cloud Functions cost and invocation count — detects runaway retries and bad deploys.
value: Daily Lambda/Azure Functions/Google Cloud Functions cost and invocation count — detects runaway retries and bad deploys.
implementation: Map usage types to invocations vs GB-sec. Alert when daily cost > 2× 7d average.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Cloud billing with serverless product codes.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: CUR, Azure meter export.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Map usage types to invocations vs GB-sec. Alert when daily cost > 2× 7d average.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cloud_billing sourcetype="aws:billing:cur" earliest=-30d
| search lineItem_ProductCode IN ("AWSLambda","AmazonSNS") OR lineItem_UsageType="*Lambda*"
| eval cost=tonumber(lineItem_UnblendedCost)
| timechart span=1d sum(cost) as serverless_cost sum(lineItem_UsageAmount) as units
```

Understanding this SPL

**Serverless Invocation Cost Trending** — Daily Lambda/Azure Functions/Google Cloud Functions cost and invocation count — detects runaway retries and bad deploys.

Documented **Data sources**: CUR, Azure meter export. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Cloud billing with serverless product codes. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cloud_billing; **sourcetype**: aws:billing:cur. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cloud_billing, sourcetype="aws:billing:cur", time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Applies an explicit `search` filter to narrow the current result set.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **cost** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1d** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.


Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (serverless $ and invocations), Table (top functions from resource tags), Single value (day-over-day %).
visualization: Line chart (serverless $ and invocations), Table (top functions from resource tags), Single value (day-over-day %).
